items.q: What is the function of the bridge in a pop song?
items.correct: To provide contrast before the final chorus, often by changing melody, key, or texture.
items.misconception: It is the same as the chorus.
items.random_wrong: It is the very first section.
items.why: Students confuse bridge with chorus because both are 'big' sections. The bridge appears once (usually after the second chorus) to provide CONTRAST and renew interest before returning.

items.q: Which orchestral family does the saxophone belong to?
items.misconception: Brass (because it is made of brass).
items.why: Students classify by material. Instrument families are defined by SOUND PRODUCTION: woodwinds use a reed or air across an edge. The sax uses a reed, so despite the brass body, it's woodwind.

items.q: How many semitones are in a perfect fifth?
items.why: Students count fifth as 'five steps' but the name refers to letter NAMES (1-2-3-4-5), not semitones. C to G spans 5 letter names but 7 semitones (C-C#-D-D#-E-F-F#-G).
It's New York. “London” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: A reused snippet of recording is called a.
items.why: Correct answer: Sample. “Riff” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many elements of hip-hop culture are listed?
items.why: Exact answer: 4. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
